佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]]

; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)

revision.text:
佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]

Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

[[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]]

'''佐紀盾列古墳群'''（さきたてなみこふんぐん）「さきたたなみ―」と読む資料もある（[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html 橿原考古学研究所サイト]など）。は、[[奈良市]]北西部、奈良丘陵の南西斜面の[[佐保川]]西岸・佐紀の地に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期後葉から中期にかけて営まれた[[ヤマト政権]]の王墓を多く含む古墳群である。佐紀古墳群とも言う。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|[[五社神古墳]]（左上）・[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（下中）・[[佐紀陵山古墳]]（下右）・[[佐紀高塚古墳]]（最下）（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[ファイル:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|[[ヒシアゲ古墳]]（左上）・[[コナベ古墳]]（下中）・[[ウワナベ古墳]]（右下）（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|佐紀盾列古墳群と他の古墳群の位置関係]] 本古墳群は、[[佐紀丘陵]]の南斜面に前期後半から中期、つまり[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]前半にかけて巨大[[前方後円墳]]が営まれた。200メートル超す巨大古墳が前期後半には3基、中期には4基みられる。これらは、初期[[ヤマト政権]]の王墓である可能性が高い。 [[奈良盆地]]の東南部のヤマト政権誕生に関わりのある[[纏向遺跡|纏向古墳群]]や[[天理市]]南部から[[桜井市]]にかけて初期ヤマト政権の大王墓を含む[[大和古墳群|大和]]・[[柳本古墳群]]が5世紀初頭には衰退し、本古墳群が4世紀後半から5世紀前半に巨大前方後円墳を営むようになった。しかし、中期末の5世紀後半から末葉になると弱小化してしまう。 形においては、撥形前方部がほとんどなくなっている。 == 名の由来 == 古墳群を代表する大型陵である「[[五社神古墳]]」（ごさしこふん）と「佐紀石塚山古墳」の治定[[陵墓|陵墓名]]がともに「狭城楯列陵」であった[[成務天皇]]の陵は『[[古事記]]』成務天皇紀では「陵は沙紀の多他那美（さきのたたなみ）にあり」と記され、『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇紀では「倭国の'''狭城盾列陵'''に葬った」とし、注に「盾列は多多那美と読む」と記されている。また、神功皇后の陵は『日本書紀』神功皇后紀にのみ「'''狭城盾列陵'''に葬った」と記されている。。 「盾（楯）列」は、周濠の形が盾型をしており、それらが北側に後円部、南側に前方部という位置でそれぞれが平行に、また東西一直線に並んでいる様子を言い表したもののようで、平城遷都後に定着した新しい言葉と推定される{{誰2|date=2010年1月}}。 なお、[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]では、古代由来の地名だけの「佐紀古墳群」としている。 == 主な古墳 == ;北西端（山陵町） *[[五社神古墳]]（ごさしこふん、[[神功皇后]]治定陵、276メートル、前期後半）2008年に墳丘1段目に限り研究者の立入り調査が認められた。 ;西（山陵町・佐紀町） *[[佐紀石塚山古墳]]（[[成務天皇]]治定陵、220メートル、前期後半） *[[佐紀陵山古墳]]（[[垂仁天皇]]妃・[[日葉酢媛命|日葉酢媛]]（ひはすひめ）治定陵、210メートル、前期後半、後円部の頂には平たく割った石を小口積みにし、高さ約70センチの石垣を矩形に巡らして内側に土を詰めた壇を造っている。2009年2月に行われた調査によれば、前方部や渡り堤の斜面に[[葺石]]が確認されている。その広さは、東西15.7メートル、南北16.51メートルと想定されている） *[[マエ塚古墳]] *[[佐紀高塚古墳]]（伝[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]陵、前方部が西向き） *[[佐紀瓢箪山古墳]]（全長96メートル、後円部径60メートル、前方部幅45メートル、後円部高さ10メートル、前方高さ7メートルの前方後円墳、周濠・葺石をもつ。1913年（大正2年）前方部西側から碧玉製琴柱形石製品3点出土。円筒埴輪・壺形埴輪。埋葬施設は未調査。前期末から中期はじめの中規模古墳。1971年（昭和46年）国の史跡に指定。） *[[猫塚古墳（奈良市）|猫塚古墳]] *[[市庭古墳]]（[[平城天皇]]治定陵、中期、250メートル、前方部削平されている） *[[神明野古墳]]（削平されている） ;東（佐紀町・法華寺町） *[[ヒシアゲ古墳]]（[[磐之媛命|磐之媛]]治定陵、218メートル、中期） *[[コナベ古墳]]（204メートル、中期） *[[ウワナベ古墳]]（265メートル、中期） *佐紀池ノ尻古墳（南北約２６０メートル、東西約２００メートルの盾形の周濠を伴った大型前方後円墳と推定。中期。平城京造営の際に完全に破壊、削平されており、現在は住宅地となっている{{Cite web |title=平城宮跡の古墳群に全長２００ｍの前方後円墳跡、被葬者はヤマト王権有力者の可能性 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20250301-OYT1T50109/ |website=読売新聞オンライン |date=2025-03-01 |access-date=2025-03-06 |language=ja}}。） ;北（歌姫町） *[[塩塚古墳]]（全長109メートル、後円部径70メートル、前方部幅55メートル、後円部高さ9メートル、前方部高さ1.5～2メートルの前方後円墳、葺石・埴輪は出土していない。中期前半の中規模古墳。1975年（昭和50年）国の史跡に指定される。 *[[オセ山古墳]]（ゲンオ塚古墳／マラ塚古墳） ;関連古墳 *[[宝来山古墳]]（[[垂仁天皇]]治定陵、227メートル） :宝来山古墳がある現・尼ヶ辻町は古代の佐紀の地に含まれないが、垂仁天皇の「菅原伏見東陵」として宮内庁書陵部畝傍監区事務所佐紀部事務所が管理しており、奈良市街周辺の古墳を一括して総称する場合、便宜的に佐紀盾列古墳群に含まれる場合がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 関連項目 == *[[百舌鳥古墳群]] *[[古市古墳群]] *[[大和古墳群]] *[[日本の大規模古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html 佐紀古墳群と松林苑] - 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所：奈良県遺跡散歩 *[http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 友史会2005年2月例会「佐紀古墳群を歩く」]（写真・図を多数収録） {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきたてなみこふんくん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群|*]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "佐紀陵山古墳}}" to "佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}}"
revision.text:
佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]

[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]

== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

revision.text:
佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]

Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
佐紀盾列古墳群
[[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] '''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Ryōzan Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Saki Ryōzan Kofun" to "Saki Misasagiyama Kofun"
revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:kk]]

The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==

The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
