'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.

However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>

It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==

; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: removing duplicates of this
revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}. [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] == Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|12=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
{{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.

The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|12=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|12=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[ja:佐紀盾列古墳群]]

It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)

== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|12=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|12=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>

It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams

revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=draft|12=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
{{draft categories|
}}

'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|12=draft|12=draft}}. [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|12=draft|12=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Saki Tatenami Kofun Group''' (佐紀盾列古墳群)<ref>Some sources read this as "Saki Tatami" ([http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/sakimisasagiyama.html Kashihara Archaeological Institute website], etc.).</ref> is a kofun group located in the northwestern part of {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the southwestern slopes of the {{ill|Nara Hills|ja|奈良丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Saho River|ja|佐保川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Constructed during the late Early to Middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it includes many royal tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
[[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 2.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom center), {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom right), and {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Sakitatenami kohungun aerial 1.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (top left), {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom center), and {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (bottom right) ({{National Air Photograph}})]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Geographical relationships of the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group and other kofun groups]]
The kofun group, situated on the southern slopes of the {{ill|Saki Hills|ja|佐紀丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was actively used during the late 4th to early 5th century, spanning the late Early to Middle Kofun period. The group includes three colossal keyhole-shaped kofun from the late Early period and four more from the Middle period. These are likely royal tombs of the early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
During this era, the Yamato Kingship transitioned from the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in the southeastern Nara Basin, to the Saki Tatenami Kofun Group, which saw the construction of massive kofun. However, by the late Middle period in the late 5th century, the group declined.
The kofun in this group are characterized by keyhole-shaped mounds without distinct paddle-shaped frontal sections.
== Origin of the Name ==
The name originates from the designations of two prominent kofun: {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, both identified by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Saki Tatenami Mausolea."<ref>For example, the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' describes Emperor Seimu's tomb as being "in Saki Tatami," while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' specifies "buried in the Saki Tatenami Mausoleum."</ref>
The term "Tatenami" (盾列, "shield alignment") may refer to the shield-like layout of the moats surrounding the keyhole-shaped kofun, which are aligned in a north-south direction with the circular rear mounds to the north and the rectangular frontal sections to the south. It likely developed as a descriptive term after the establishment of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|ja|平城京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."
== Major Kofun ==
; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kashikoken.jp/iseki/iseki_sanpo/course07.html] - {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm "Walking Through the Saki Kofun Group"] - 2005 event photos and diagrams
{{draft categories|
}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nara City]] [[Category:Saho-Saki Route]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] }}

Some institutions, such as the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, use the historical name "Saki Kofun Group."

; Northwestern Area (Yamamori-cho) * {{ill|Gosashi Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 276 m, late Early Kofun period)
; Western Area (Yamamori-cho, Saki-cho) * {{ill|Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 220 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Ryoyama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, consort of Emperor Suinin, 210 m, late Early Kofun period) * {{ill|Saki Takatsuka Kofun|ja|佐紀高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (associated with {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, direction of frontal section unknown)
; Eastern Area (Saki-cho, Hokkeji-cho) * {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified as the tomb of {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 218 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (204 m, Middle Kofun period) * {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (265 m, Middle Kofun period)
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of large kofun in Japan|ja|日本の大規模古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
